name: Ascent
description: Open mid with mechanical doors. Strong for operators and long-range agents.
notes: Mid control is crucial. Door control adds strategic depth. Good for almost any comp.

name: Breeze
description: Massive open spaces. Long sightlines. Favors operators and long-range utility.
notes: Viper and Sova shine here. Long-range operators dominate. Tight hallways on mid.

name: Fracture
description: Attacker-sided H-shape map. Defenders get pressured from both flanks simultaneously.
notes: Strong attack-sided map. Mobile agents and flank-aware sentinels are key. Door splits the map.

name: Haven
description: Only 3-site map. Defenders are stretched thin. High rotation speed needed.
notes: 3 bomb sites means defenders must rotate fast. Controllers and info-gathering agents shine.

name: Lotus
description: 3-site map with rotating doors and breakable walls. Complex rotations for both sides.
notes: Doors add unique rotation dynamics. Strong for utility-heavy comps with good communication.

name: Pearl
description: Defense-sided underwater city. Long mid is crucial. No mechanics or gimmicks.
notes: Mid control is dominant. Strong sentinel presence helps defend the long mid.

name: Split
description: Defense-sided vertical map. Many close corners. Rope traversal is key.
notes: Very defense-sided. Sentinels and flank watchers dominate. Vertical play with Raze/Jett.

name: Bind
description: No mid — two one-way teleporters connect the map. Attack-sided with flank potential.
notes: Teleporters create flank opportunities. No mid lane means direct A or B executes. Raze is dominant here.

name: Icebox
description: Defense-sided industrial map. Vertical play, ziplines, and tight corridors.
notes: Ziplines enable vertical repositioning. Strong for KJ and Viper. Sage wall is powerful here.

name: Sunset
description: Mid-focused map with a mechanical door on B. Balanced with strong mid control.
notes: Mid control via B-link is crucial. Door mechanic on B site adds strategic depth like Ascent.

name: Abyss
description: No walls on the edges — fall off and die. Unique verticality with no spike drop.
notes: Edge pressure is unique — agents with mobility or ledge denial are powerful. Operator and long-range dominate.
